foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: GERD is the primary indication — understanding reflux pathophysiology and failed medical therapy criteria is essential

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Fundoplication mechanically augments the LES anti-reflux barrier — LES pressure dynamics determine surgical approach

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Hiatal anatomy, short gastric vessels, and vagal trunks are critical operative landmarks

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Fundoplication is the cornerstone anti-reflux procedure within the surgical GERD management algorithm

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Partial fundoplication (Dor/Toupet) is routinely added to Heller myotomy to prevent post-myotomy reflux

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Choice of partial vs full wrap depends on esophageal motility — achalasia mandates partial fundoplication

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Pre-operative manometry determines wrap type — full (Nissen) for normal motility, partial for impaired peristalsis
